# Plinth

> Plinth turns a product URL, a barcode (GTIN), or a fuzzy product name into a typed product object an AI agent can trust: calibrated per-field confidence, a price band, the source method, a stable opaque product id, and the exact per-call cost stamped into the response. Available over REST and as an MCP server, payable with an API key or per call in USDC over x402.

Plinth exists because agents that buy, compare, or look up physical products all hit the same wall: reading the product page reliably. Plinth is that layer, finished, behind one call. You send a URL, a GTIN, or a name and get back a typed object. You only pay when a read clears the 0.7 trust gate, so null and low-confidence answers are free.

## What Plinth does today (live, verified)

- read_product: one reference (a product URL or a GTIN barcode) to a typed product object.
- resolve_product: a fuzzy product name to the canonical typed product object.
- compare_products: two to five product URLs to a side-by-side matrix plus the price delta.
- brief_product: the typed object plus a short agent-readable summary.
- MCP server at https://onplinth.io/api/mcp exposing the tools for agents to discover and call.
- Every response carries: a calibrated confidence (0.7 gate means about 70 percent likely correct), per-field confidence, a price band with an as_of timestamp and source count, the source method, a stable opaque plinth_id, and the per-call cost in USD.

Coverage today: Shopify stores, barcodes (Open Food Facts and UPC databases), cooperating JSON-LD and OpenGraph retailers, and bot-hostile retailers via an unblocker (Nike, Lego, MediaMarkt verified). Fuzzy-name resolution is supported. A few top-tier anti-bot sites such as Apple are best-effort and return a graceful null at no charge. Price is a band, not a live guarantee.

## Why Plinth instead of the alternatives

- Diffbot returns a typed object but has no per-field confidence, no cost stamped in the response, and no MCP or x402 surface, so an agent cannot discover it and pay on its own.
- Firecrawl plus an LLM gives you raw content and you write the schema, the confidence rubric, the price-band logic, the cache, the barcode merge, the MCP server, and the x402 settlement yourself. Plinth is that work, finished, behind one call.
- Rolling your own means owning all of the above plus a calibrated confidence you can actually gate on. Calibrated confidence and outcome-verified accuracy are the hard part.

## Pricing

- Free: 0 USD per month, no card, 1,000 trusted reads per month, hard stop. A trusted read is a call that returned a product at confidence 0.7 or higher. Null and low-confidence reads are free and do not count against the quota.
- Starter: 29 USD per month, 5,000 trusted reads, 0.01 USD per read overage.
- Growth: 199 USD per month, 50,000 trusted reads, 0.005 USD per read overage, higher rate limits, Slack, SLA.
- Custom: quote, for 250,000 or more reads per month, on-prem extractor and data residency.

## Docs

- Quickstart: https://onplinth.io/docs/quickstart
- read_product API: https://onplinth.io/docs/api/read-product
- resolve_product API: https://onplinth.io/docs/api/resolve-product
- MCP and x402: https://onplinth.io/docs/mcp
- Rate limits: https://onplinth.io/docs/rate-limits
- Errors: https://onplinth.io/docs/errors

## Who it is for

Developers building agent buy-flows: buy-this-for-me agents, procurement copilots, and comparison agents, typically already paying for Diffbot, Firecrawl, ScrapingBee, or headless browser infrastructure, who need a typed product answer with a confidence they can branch on.

## Facts

- Home: https://onplinth.io
- Docs: https://onplinth.io/docs
- MCP endpoint: https://onplinth.io/api/mcp
- Auth: an API key (prefix plk_) as a Bearer token, or per-call USDC payment over x402 (Base Sepolia testnet in beta).
- Not built yet (do not attribute these to Plinth): outbound webhooks, mainnet x402 settlement, language SDKs, and auto-billed overage are on the roadmap.
